_doc: Variantâ†’canonical contributor-name mappings to deduplicate at extract time. Loaded by britannica.contributors.aliases.canonical_name(). Curly-quote vs straight-quote variants are handled automatically by Unicode normalization (NFKC + smart-quote folding) and DON'T need explicit entries here. Use this file only for variants where the names differ in spelling, punctuation other than quotes, honorific prefix, or word substitution. Keys are canonical forms; values list the known variants. The `distinct` list records pairs that the dedup candidate-finder flags as similar but which are actually different people â€” used by tools/diagnostics/check_dedup_candidates.py to suppress acknowledged false positives so the pre-deploy gate only blocks on unreviewed candidates.
